lesson.title: Give the ball room to breathe
lesson.body_md:
## Why spacing wins

Watch the best offenses. They don't run fancy plays. They just stand in the right spots. When everyone is spread out, drives have somewhere to kick. The paint stays open. Defenders can't help.

```takeaway
Bad spacing = clogged paint = no offense. Spread the floor.
```

## The 5 spots

Five players, five spots:

1. Top of the key (slot)
2. Both wings (45 degrees out)
3. Both corners

```tip
Think of a five-pointed star. One person on each tip. That's perfect spacing.
```

## The spacing rule

```coach
Stay 12 to 15 feet from the next teammate.
```

Closer, and one defender can guard two of you. Farther, and the pass takes too long.

## During a drive

```reveal
Q: Your teammate drives. You're the strong-side corner. What do you do?
A: Drop deeper into the corner. Don't lift up — that pulls your defender right into the lane.
```

```reveal
Q: Your teammate drives. You're at the top of the key. What do you do?
A: Slide to the opposite slot. Now you're a reversal pass if the drive gets cut off.
```

## Test yourself

```quiz
Q: You're standing right next to your teammate at the wing. What's wrong?
- Nothing, looks fine ✗
- One defender is guarding both of you ✓
- You're too far apart ✗
Why: Bunching up means one defender can help on both. Spread out.
```

```quiz
Q: Your point guard drives at you on the wing. What do you do?
- Stand still ✗
- Lift to the slot to give an outlet ✓
- Cut into the lane in front of them ✗
Why: Lifting opens a kick-out. Cutting clogs the drive. Standing still lets your defender help.
```

## What kills spacing

```mistake
Drifting toward the ball. Now your defender can sag in and help.
Fix: when in doubt, drift AWAY from the ball.
```

```mistake
Standing on the baseline in the corner. The corner is gold — but only if your foot is INSIDE the line.
```

## 3 questions before every move

1. Am I dragging my defender into the paint? (If yes, stay.)
2. Will my new spot help the driver? (If yes, move.)
3. Is somebody already there? (If yes, pick a different spot.)

```takeaway
Pass and move. Drive and space. Never stand where you started.
```

Now try it in practice.
